next_actions: Use the CYINT IP proof packet for provisional app 64/068,121 as the filed disclosure reference.
Before public paper release, compare manuscript content against the filed specification/drawings/CPL appendix and hold any new technical matter for a follow-on filing decision.
Route the terminal human and additional LLM review packets before publication-facing release.

Keep all publication claims inside the automated terminal claim boundary: bounded Stage 4 real-noisy-hardware packet, no broad quantum-attention, full-transformer, cross-backend, or hardware-superiority claims.
Track the 2027-05-18 nonprovisional, PCT, and foreign-priority follow-on deadline in CYINT IP.

Submit the next Stage 4 replication only after explicitly selecting the prepared no-tuning target `ibm_kingston`; do not change packet rows, circuit family, shots, metrics, thresholds, budget cap, or stop rules.
risks: The provisional submission proof is catalogued, but the formal USPTO Filing Receipt still should be docketed when issued.
Public disclosure outside the filed specification/drawings/CPL appendix could create new-matter and global-filing risk.
The real noisy-hardware success claim is bounded to IBM ibm_fez job d84jbq00bvlc73d4krr0, packet qrope-hardware-73c61893576297ff, and its recorded calibration window.
Transformer integration should be disclosed as supported or prophetic embodiments unless transformer-scale validation later passes deterministic gates.
Replication must use the fixed packet, unchanged gates, budget cap, and stop rules; the prepared target is ibm_kingston, but no replication hardware job has been submitted.

First-pass search found broad quantum RoPE, quantum positional encoding, quantum self-attention, and quantum transformer claims crowded; draft around the narrowed phase-wrap scoring, circuit, and deterministic validation spine.
The formal claims draft is intentionally broad for provisional preservation and should be pruned before any nonprovisional filing to manage examination, restriction, and excess-claim risk.
The Section 103 rebuttal workbook prepares arguments and fallback amendments but does not confirm that obviousness rejections will be avoided or overcome.
